name: The Elven Dune-Raider
concept: You run the open waste on legs that never tire, raiding and trading and vanishing before the dust settles. Your tribe's oaths are the whole of your honor; an outsider's word is wind. The Free Legion is still learning which of those you offer them.
duty: Keep faith with the tribe and you are never alone in the waste. Break an elf-oath and you are worse than dead to them, and to yourself.

look.label: Dress
options: next to nothing; speed is all

gear.label: Waste-kit
options: a trader's pack of light goods
trophies and trail-signs of your tribe

gear.label: Oath-token
options: a debt of blood owed to you
an oath you have not yet kept

moves.name: Run the Waste
moves.trigger: When you cross the open desert at the elven pace, or run down a quarry, or vanish into the dunes.

results.success: You get there, or get away, faster than anything on foot can follow.
results.failure: The waste beats you this time: too far, too dry, or too watched.
advantage: Hold 1 Stride (spend: bring others along at your pace / arrive unseen / find water or a landmark on the way / choose the ground where you stop).
You read the land ahead — where the danger and the water lie.
threat: The run costs you: thirst and strain, a setback die until you rest.
You are spotted, or you outrun your own supply.
results.triumph: You cover impossible ground and arrive fresh.
results.despair: The waste turns on you — a storm, a predator, a dead end with no way back.

hold.name: Stride
spend: Bring companions along at your pace, this once.
Find water, forage, or a landmark on the way.
Choose the ground where the run ends.

moves.name: Tribe Oaths
moves.trigger: When you call on your raiding tribe for shelter, arms, or riders (Invoke Patron, core.md):
moves.text: They answer if you have kept faith, and add 1 Debt. But the tribe holds outsiders — the Legion among them — in open contempt, and an elf-oath broken makes you nothing to them. Weigh whose oaths you keep.

moves.name: Raider's Bow
moves.trigger: When you strike from the saddle or the ridge and are gone before they close.

results.success: Your shot lands, and you are already moving.
results.failure: You are drawn into the fight you meant to avoid.
advantage: Keep the range: they cannot reach you.
threat: You loose your last good arrows.
They mark your line of retreat.
results.triumph: You break them without ever letting them near.
results.despair: They have a way to catch you, and you just showed them where you are.

moves.name: Desert Craft
moves.trigger: When you read the waste for water, weather, trail, or threat.

results.success: The desert tells you what you need: where the water is, what the sky will do, who passed and when.
results.failure: The waste keeps its secrets, and the not-knowing will cost you.
advantage: Enough foresight to guide the whole Legion true.
You spot the danger before it spots you.
threat: What you read is already changing.
Reading the trail, you left one of your own.
results.triumph: You know the land as if you shaped it.
results.despair: You misread the sky, and lead everyone into it.

moves.name: Fleet and Unseen
moves.trigger: When you move fast and hidden across open or broken ground.

results.success: You go where you like, quick and unseen.
results.failure: You are seen, or the ground defeats your speed.
advantage: Take one other with you, just as hidden.
threat: You are quick but not quiet, or quiet but not quick — pick your cost.
results.triumph: You pass through them like wind through grass.
results.despair: You run straight into what you were hiding from.

moves.name: An Elf's Bargain
moves.trigger: When you strike a sharp trade with an outsider:
moves.text: Take a boost die to drive the price your way. But an elf's bargain is a famous thing: they will expect the letter of the deal and no more, and so will you. The GM plays every gap you leave.

bonds: ____ ran the waste beside me once and kept pace; among my people that means everything.
____ thinks my tribe are thieves and killers, and is not entirely wrong.
I gave ____ an elf-oath, and I have not decided whether I will keep it.
____ trusts me at their back, and my tribe would call them a fool for it.
I owe ____ a debt of water, which among my people is a debt of life.
communityTie: The Free Legion needs a scout who owns the waste; ____ argued to bring an elf among them despite the muttering.
Your tribe would raid the Legion as soon as aid it; ____ is the reason you steer them away.
You could vanish into the dunes any night and leave the slow-footed Legion behind; ____ is why you are still here.

tracks.name: Debt
tracks.note: At 6 the tribe makes a major demand, then resets to 3.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Stride by 1.
Know a range: name a stretch of waste you can cross blind, in any weather.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Prove your faith: raise your standing with the tribe by 1 through a kept oath.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Choose the Legion over the tribe: break with your people for good, resolving the fallout in play.
Bring the tribe in: broker a true alliance, founding a shared Bastion (a desert waystation) for Legion and tribe both.
